lede: A look at December 5 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Józef Piłsudski and André-Pierre Gignac, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: Westroads Mall shooting
events.desc: Westroads Mall shooting: Nineteen-year-old Robert A. Hawkins kills nine people, including himself, with a WASR-10 at a Von Maur department store in Omaha, Nebraska.

events.title: Egypt
events.desc: Egypt breaks diplomatic relations with Syria, Libya, Algeria, Iraq and South Yemen in retaliation to preventing President Anwar el-Sadat from pursuing negotiations with Israel at the Tripoli confer.

events.title: Great Smog of London
events.desc: Beginning of the Great Smog in London. A cold fog combines with air pollution and brings the city to a standstill for four days. Later, a Ministry of Health report estimates 4,000 fatalities as a result of it.

events.title: Finland
events.desc: World War II: Great Britain declares war on Finland, Hungary and Romania.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: In the Battle of Moscow, Georgy Zhukov launches a massive Soviet counter-attack against the German army.

events.title: Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition
events.desc: The Imperial Trans-Antarctic Expedition begins in an attempt to make the first land crossing of Antarctica.

events.title: Seven Years' War
events.desc: Seven Years' War: Battle of Leuthen: Frederick II of Prussia leads Prussian forces to a decisive victory over Austrian forces under Prince Charles Alexander of Lorraine.

events.title: Pope Innocent VIII
events.desc: Pope Innocent VIII issues the Summis desiderantes affectibus, a papal bull that deputizes Heinrich Kramer and Jacob Sprenger as inquisitors to root out alleged witchcraft in Germany.

events.title: 1456 Central Italy earthquakes
events.desc: The first of two earthquakes measuring Mw 7.2 strikes Italy, causing extreme destruction and killing upwards of 70,000 people.

events.title: Cicero
events.desc: Cicero gives the fourth and final of the Catiline Orations.

births.name: Józef Piłsudski
births.line: Polish field marshal and politician, 15th Prime Minister of Poland

births.name: Walt Disney
births.line: American animator, director, producer, and screenwriter, co-founded The Walt Disney Company

births.name: Martin Van Buren
births.line: American lawyer and politician, 8th President of the United States

births.name: Werner Heisenberg
births.line: German physicist and academic, Nobel Prize laureate

deaths.text: Nelson Mandela, South African lawyer and politician, 1st President of South Africa, Nobel Prize laureate
Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart, Austrian composer and musician
Ramon Berenguer II, Count of Barcelona, Count of Barcelona from 1076 to 1082

observances.text: Christian feast day: Clement of Alexandria (Episcopal Church)
Christian feast day: Justinian of Ramsey Island
